title: Signal [1] is defined more than once.   (Example included)
body:
Hi, 
I would like to provide some details. 
I am using the following script to synthesize the circuit to generate the blif file. 
**yosys -p "hierarchy; proc -global_arst reset; flatten;  techmap; ice40_opt -unlut; opt_clean; abc -script +strash;ifraig;scorr;retime;strash; clean; write_blif"**

I get the following blif file. 
model example
.inputs a
.outputs zro_zro zro_not zro_buf zro_one not_zro not_not not_buf not_one buf_zro buf_not buf_buf buf_one one_zro one_not one_buf one_one magic_inverter
.names $false
.names $true
1
.names $undef
.names a inverter
0 1
.names a buf_buf
1 1
.names a buf_not
1 1
.names a buf_one
1 1
.names a buf_zro
1 1
.names inverter not_buf
1 1
.names inverter not_not
1 1
.names inverter not_one
1 1
.names inverter not_zro
1 1
.names $true one_buf
1 1
.names $true one_not
1 1
.names $true one_one
1 1
.names $true one_zro
1 1
.names $false zro_buf
1 1
.names $false zro_not
1 1
.names $false zro_one
1 1
.names $false zro_zro
1 1
.end
For Miter computation using ABC I give this blif file to abc, I am facing the error Line 6: Signal "[1]" is defined more than once.

I hope I may be able to make this issue understandable.
Thanks @cliffordwolf in advance. If you require anything else, please do let me know, I would like to email you. 
Thanks
